description: Storytelling approach that traces product origins and sustainability journey from source to consumer, emphasizing transparency and environmental consciousness in food and beverage.
structural_recipe: Lead with origin story of product (e.g., coffee bean journey from farm). Use bilingual captions (Arabic-English) to reach broader audience. Layer discovery narrative—position sustainability as something customer learns progressively. Include visual progression from raw ingredient to finished product. Use emotive language about depth of process. Invite consumer to engage with 'deeper understanding' of sourcing.
why_it_works: Saudi consumers show growing interest in premium sourcing and ethical consumption. Storytelling connects emotional narrative to product value, elevating commodity items to meaningful purchases. Bilingual approach signals global brand sophistication while respecting local language preference. The 'journey' framing taps into values of heritage and tradition while appearing modern and conscious.
transplantation_caveats: Sustainability messaging must be authentic—greenwashing is rejected by informed Saudi audiences
Verify environmental claims are verifiable or risk reputation damage
Some conservative segments may prioritize quality/taste over sustainability narrative
Bilingual execution requires cultural fluency in both Arabic and English tone
cultural_constraints: Sustainability framing should complement rather than replace quality/heritage messaging
Islamic values around stewardship of resources can reinforce narrative authenticity
